author: @KatFanFam
text: The Swiss guy was late to the party. Alexander Bain (1810–1877), a Scottish inventor and engineer developed the "earth battery" in his lab in Edinburgh around 1841, by burying zinc (anode) and copper (cathode) plates roughly one meter apart in moist soil.  Bain was first to invent and patent the electric clock. He created the first fax machine, known as Bain's facsimile. Bain also installed the railway telegraph lines between Edinburgh and Glasgow.  Then stateside there was Nathan Beverly Stubblefield (November 22, 1860 – March 28, 1928)  an American inventor best known for his wireless telephone work. Self-described as a "practical farmer, fruit grower and electrician", he received widespread attention in early 1902 when he gave a series of public demonstrations of a battery-operated wireless telephone.  Stubblefield's earth battery, patented in 1898 (U.S. Patent 600,457), was a device designed to generate electricity using moisture and minerals in the soil. It consisted of an insulated copper wire and bare iron wire wound together as a coil, which, when buried, created a continuous, low-voltage current for his wireless telephone experiments.

author: @thomthumbe
text: While a bit off topic, during WWI, wired intercoms (as opposed to extremely primitive radio in those days) often used only one wire.  Since two conductors are needed, they would use one wire and then they would pound a long ground rod into the Earth, and that worked….at least sort of.  There was LOTS of noise (which ties in nicely with the topic of this video) and it was found that since everybody used a ground rod, it became fairly easy to listen in on other conversations, including your enemy.  In other words, the second wire for everyone was the same “wire” conductor….or the Earth.  These stray earth voltages played real havoc with communication requirements.  Especially during rain or snow storms.  Solar effects on the Earth were also a large player, but that was completely unknown in those days.

author: @blzz42
text:
Curious how that works. I wonder if being near running water or the water retention in the soil would be a factor to consider.

I remember learning about Teslas tower and how he was inspired by the how the pyramids were constructed in Egypt.

They had a reservoir of running water underneath the pyramids, Where the water flowing in and out would create some sort of pressure vacuum in the pyramid itself and transferring energy to the quartz via the piezoelectric effect.

I remember reading about Tesla and how he may have constructed small aqueducts underneath the Wardencliff tower to perform the same function, with these large granite stones in the ground as connection points.

Makes you think maybe he was working on this earth battery type thing, and found a way to multiply the energy that came from the ground.

Also makes you think what you could possibly do using copper wire, like perhaps by coiling the copper wire you could step up the voltage a bit.

Idk I’m no electrical engineer but I’m endlessly fascinated by the subject, makes me want to experiment myself.

author: @OlderThanDirt-ii3rp
text: The railroad ran the telegraph buy burying a 3 foot square 1/2 inch copper plate on one side of the tracks and a 3 foot square 1/2 zinc plate on the other.  A wire from each went to the telegraph. That produced 1.5 volts and the large surface area gave sufficient amperage to run the telegraph.  The question is how far apart do these "Cells" need to be to add voltage.  4 such cells should give you 6 volts and a good bit of amperage.  These days a voltage doubler transformer ain't no step for a stepper.

author: @thedolphin5428
text:
Not to contradict or slag your video ...

"Energy generation" is a misnomer. It is actually energy HARVESTING followed by transformation into the usable form we want. 

According to both commomsense AND physics, energy cannot be made. The same quantum of energy in the universe has been there since day one. It is just distributed in many many forms, everywhere. It can only be transformed from one form into another -- ie solar radiation into electrical voltage; nuclear energy into a steam turbine electrical energy or a bomb; petrol hydrocarbons into vehicular motion; gravity into hydro power; food into metabolism and human activity.

Which is why fusion energy is a joke -- it takes MORE to make less, same as for all energy transformations -- they have inefficiencies.

Humans try to harness one f9rce from another. Fossil fuels have been massively convenient. New forms may never been as efficient.

author: @Bones469
text:
Very pleased to have this pop up in my feed.   I have toyed with these ideas as well along with using trees and maybe even a corrosion based system for people near the ocean where you could cascade the salt water through boxes with dissimilar metals, maybe like a little labyrinth or something.  

There was a story or tale of an experimenter guy our here in Utah that was a scout master that lived about 100 years ago.  The old timers claimed he would put out probes to light the area on campouts. 

My thought on this has always gone a couple directions.  I always thought that if telluric, there may be more voltage be creating distance between the probes to better exploit the ground current differentials. I have done some experiments here but I live in the proximity of some high tension lines that may be creating induction on my conductors. I need new probes for my old O-scopes to look at the wave form but now they probably have soft o-scope good enough for what I would use it for.... I need to check that... Wish and Temu.. lol 

This brings up another point.  I have suspected that if done correctly you should be able to somehow exploit the magnetic flux of the earth.  IIRC is that about 7.8Hz?   If so creating the correct LC tank may allow for better extrapolation of the energy.  Now that we have 1 farad caps for car stereos and things it seems like it would be possible.  Before I was toying with the idea of tuning "Wine Bottle Caps" popular amongst Tesla Coil enthusiasts, Of course I have some other ideas how I could use such a capacitance bank as we move deeper and deeper into low-voltage solid state devices... for which I won't mention publicly here as it might get the wrong kind of attention... lol

Anyway I might revisit these earth and tree batteries and pair the experiment up with a "Joule Thief" style circuit as I think you could produce usable lighting in a long term grid down scenario.   Always keep extra Diodes and transistors in your Faraday junk box.. just sayin'... lol

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text:
40-50 Full Milliamperes w/ Magnesium and Carbon One Cell
Materials:  one magnesium rod 12 inch length 5/16 inch diameter, carbon fiber cloth 12 x 6 feet, one sheet of paper, electrical tape and water bath.  Wrap magnesium rod with paper to insulate it from carbon.  Then roll rod with the full length of carbon fiber fabric.  Tape to hold.  Be sure to leave a bit of the magnesium rod exposed to attach your voltmeter.  This gave a full 40+ milliamperes.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: I screwed up about the texture.  It is area of the tall jar of water and the 12-inch rod of magnesium.  I never expected to see 5 milliamperes and jumped to a wrong conclusion.   I performed a bad science experiment by making changes before duplicating the experiment of someone else.  I shaved the magnesium first, with hopes of making a new modern type of cell.  I then used that textured rod which produced much higher amperage.  5/16" diameter rod.  However, on the second attempt with a smooth rod the 4 milliamperes was still true.  I then learned proximity of rods makes higher amperage.  The large vessel and rods made that possible to determine.  That is a 100% and I tested that several times.  The amps would drop into the 2 range and as I brought them closer would climb up into the 6-7 but settle back down in the 4-ma range.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: Obtaining 4-5 Full Milliamperes with Magnesium, Carbon and water.  Two rods place in tap water.  Before immersion the magnesium rod, take a hack saw blade or file and texture the length of the rod with deep triangular ridges.  Electrons love pointed edges to gather and launch from on metals.  The surface area is also increased which also is a natural boost to amperage.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: The modern alkaline AA battery.  Cut one open.  You will find that the battery today is mostly carbon.  The center of the battery is a paper tube filled with metal powder.  I think it is magnesium.  The battery is moist and works using a base liquid or alkaline liquid.  It produces 1.5 Volts and 3 amps.  The manufacturing is known for earth batteries.  We simply switch the base for water, which should produce 2 volts and .8 amps.  The switch to water hopefully will make the battery last longer.  Recharging could be as simple as changing the water.  If the metal is battery part that is being "destroyed", then we can recycle.  It is simple chemistry to recapture the used metal.  The interesting thing after I cut open the battery, the metal powder seemed to look fine.  Also, knowing that an increase in surface area will generate more amps, the powdered metal is probably the best way to insure mass area in a small space like the core of a AA battery.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text:
Telluric Matrix Soup Theory
I have a theory that the telluric matrix, the material the dissimilar metals are placed is a great place to experiment.  Everyone else is changing out metals, but them matrix, soil water etc. can be improved.  I saw a glimpse of that when I sprinkled graphite on top of the water and the battery improved.  I think some of the non-metallic and potentially some of the metallic elements can be added to the matrix to create and electron soup if you will.  Just a theory, but I think I am on to something.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: I am going to try some silicon wafers also, just for shits and giggles.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text:
Polymer Clay Experiment
This man made clay will produce a very small current.  It will not dry out when exposed.  It smells of vinyl, and is basically an insulation material.  I have no idea how it works.  Luckily, I bought a 4 color pack.  The additives used to produce the clay changes the voltages.  This is part of my alloy theory.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: Since carbon works, I would recommend checking all the safe and stable non-metallic elements with metals.  Just like you check the metals against carbon.  We may even be able to compound the non metallic elements to create a super cathode or anode.  Which ever the hell carbon is.  I just ordered me some carbon rods.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text:
I do not know which metals will produce a super alloy or if it is even possible.  But I want to know.  How come we cannot turn to a telluric chapter in a Chemistry textbook.  What elements make electrical currents.  I have produced two dry reactions, no soil, clay or water.  
1.  Iron copper and zinc: I hooked copper and zinc to an old cast iron pot.  Very small current.  
2. Copper, zinc and graphite.  I poured graphite powder into a paper cup and placed copper and zinc wires and nails.  It worked.  That is what spurred me to sprinkle it on the water experiment.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: Theoretical thought: Alloys.  Do alloys allow for the voltages or amperages of the elemental metals to add up in an earth battery.  If we were to create a super alloy made of equal parts of aluminum and magnesium, would the battery generate 3 volts when paired with carbon?

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: Distilled water gives volts but zero amps.

author: @DonaldMcKenzie-nn4pw
text: You have to try this experiment.  Use tap water no salt and two metals.  I used copper and a zinc nail.  Hook up your meter.  Then sprinkle the top of the water with graphite powder.  The amps went from .2 ma and jumped up to .45 milliamperes.  The volts climbed by about 27% from .7 V to .975 volts.

author: @MichaelGlass1983
text:
Stefan Marinov, leader of the European free energy movement. Made great strides towards creating zero point energy. Fell out of a window.

Joseph Newman, American inventor, creates perpetual motion free energy machine. Fought the government for close to a decade trying to get his device patented. Even with a working prototype his invention was fully suppressed. 

John Mullen, nuclear physicist. Working on free energy. Died of arsenic poisoning. 

Dmitry Petronov, invented plasma battery that powered his home for 14 months. Disappeared. 

Zachary Warfield, invented his own version of the plasma battery. Dies in mysterious boating accident the same year.

Eugene Mallove, physicist and expert in cold fusion, claimed to have working prototype of free energy device. In 2004 the day before he was to give public announcement... he was beaten to death.

Arie Degeus, patented free energy technology. He was taking a flight to meet investors to fund his research. Found dead in his car at the airport.

Rory Johnson created cold fusion laser activated motor. Planned demonstration of four vehicles using this technology. US dept of Energy places restraining order against this technology to prevent  publication. Dies soon after. 

Mark Tomion, physicist, patents technology called Star Drive that uses zero point energy. In 2009 develops working prototype. Soon after dies from cardic arrest. All his research went missing. 

Stanley Meyer developed engine that ran on water. Dies during meeting with two potential investors. Official cause of death, cerebral hemorrhage. His last words were "I was poisoned."

James Allen, documentary film maker, exposes the government, Lockheed Martin, general electric, Boeing, and all the usual suspects in a massive conspiracy to cover up and prevent the disclosure of free energy systems that would radically change society. While still editing the film he was diagnosed with an aggressive form of cancer. He was dead in 3 months. 

And most recently Aaron Salter Jr. was shot and killed during a mass shooting while working as a security guard at Tops Friendly Markets in Buffalo, New York, on May 14 2022. The news never mentions how Salter was working on water powered engines for his company, Hydrogen Technologies LLC. I guess it's just another coincidence? Or maybe an assassination staged as a mass shooting?

Suicided. Disappeared. Suppressed. Mocked. Maligned. Infected with cancer from low frequency microwave pulse weapons. What a world!

author: @MichaelQShaw
text:
Earth battery? It just simple galvanic cell, redox with an anode and cathode. It’s the basis of alkaline batteries and Cathodic protection. Look up the galvanic series chart there is a good one on corrosion pedia for the different materials voltages everyone.

Your highest voltage difference will be between a magnesium -1.6V high purity anode and a carbon graphite +.2V cathode, it’s a 1.8v difference which is why you got 2V, only problem is that it will only work until the magnesium anode is spent. It is the electro-chemical process that causes corrosion. 

Yes, it also works better with a salt electrolyte otherwise a more conducive electrolyte in the soil around both electrodes. It doesn’t work when the soil is dry, it’s also why you don’t need to use Cathodic protection in frozen ground that never thaws, current just simply cannot flow in frozen ground.

It is surface area of the anode and cathode and the material of both.  You can increase that surface area by digging a deeper and wider hole, place the anode in the middle and backfill with coke breeze, just like a Cathodic protection technician would do. 

Oxidation reaction is the loss of electrons which can happen as rust (exposed to air) when metal loses electrons to oxygen in the air or to an anode in the ground in a galvanic cell. 

Electrons move through the electrical connection from the anode loosing electrons to the cathode making the cathode more negative. At the same time positive ions move towards the cathode in the electrolyte.

Oxidation, anode, loss of electrons (becoming more positive)
Reduction, cathode, gain of electrons (becoming more negative)

The anode is sacrificed and chemically changes through oxidation and slowly disappears protecting the cathode by sacrificially giving it its electrons.

Telluric currents would be difficult to use, they constantly change, are unpredictable, vary with the variations in the earth and even are effected by the sun.

Also the current goes in to the earth in gradient shells, just like a ground rod or even lightning dissipating in voltage gradient shells into the earth.

NACE International rocks, you can get a Corrosion Technician Certification through them. Cool stuff. Not free energy though. 🤣
Love your channel.

But considering this law, there is no energy surplus coming from the Earth that we would be able to tap into without greatly affecting it. author: @RickarooCarew
text:
✌️ let's think about what we know señor Robert... about the Earth .. there's less than a hundred kilometers of solid rock... and another 12,000km of mostly semi molten mantle between your feet and the other side of the Planet.. with the core in the middle of course.. but it's mostly semi molten mantle... it's semi because of the pressure... it's molten because it's radioactive... most people live in places with top soil... an organic detritus from living things where other living things can grow... I live in the desert and... I'm a lot of things.. but I call myself a geologist... my dad taught reactor physics ... he actually told me this stuff back in the early 60s... he was trying to blow up the World at the time. .. so it's not new information..  the rest of the inorganic solids on the surface are from the semi molten mantle.. the background radiation we adjust for in a Geiger counter is exactly because all rocks and dirt are slightly radioactive... there are two types of primary radiation in this stuff.. alpha emissions from the decay or radioactive isotopes through nuclear fission.. spitting out a helium nucleus at something very close to light speed.. or.. we find beta emissions from the radioactive fission of a neutron.. spitting out the mass of an electron at light speed.. carbon 14 does this regularly.. C14 then has another proton and becomes N14.. the next element up the periodic table.. that type of growth is the point of a nuclear reactor.. that's not how they make electricity though.. the reactor boils water, steam turns turbines and generators.. 
but... I use graphite crucibles in the lab.. mostly C12.. I had a roll of copper sheet inside one as a convenient place to keep the roll from unrolling.. I work with several flavors of volcanic ash.. from which I extract platinum and palladium mixed with gold.. platinum and palladium in covalent bonds rather than metallic bonds because it forms deep slowly.. held together by the gold between the unit cells with metallic bonds... this creates a superconductor for magnetic energy.. when I test for resistance I don't get a zero reading.. I get a negative number... less than zero resistance.. every time... definitely repeatable in your lab... 
platinum has a radioactive isotope called Pt190.. which is why natural platinum crystals are usually grey instead of white metal... so the radioactive component is mostly beta emissions.. similar to plutonium... but not so energetic... and there's a process from having too few neutrons which creates alpha emissions.. to too many neutrons which creates beta emissions and platinum becomes gold.. but the half life is measured in hundreds of billions of years..  and it's primarily beta... electrons at light speed... 
so.. with a cylinder type crucible... place a bi-metal sheet... I would use platinum and palladium.. with the palladium facing into the container and a rod running up the middle as the electrode... palladium wire is easy to make.. fill with the gold colored dust from the side of any dirt road... I have lived in Europe but not the UK... but I am pretty sure it's all the same stuff... screen out rocks.. I screen to 300 microns... add hydrogen peroxide... I think it will work Robert.. but I am trying pretty hard to die from radiation poisoning at the moment... why don't you give it a go... see what happens brother
and
Merry Christmas from me and mine to thee and thine

author: @davidrabold2839
text:
You do some really cool, crazy stuff. I enjoy your work. 
Question; how do you think it would react if instead of putting this in series, if you would connect them + to- and- to +. I've not done experiments with DC? So I don't know about the properties. What I was thinking of is a 24v battery system that uses (2) 12v batteries to achieve the 24v amount.

replies.author: @KuptisOriginal
replies.text: Connecting + to - ( -|+-|+-|+-|+ where -|+ is a group of rods/battery) is the same as connecting them in series in which voltages add.  If you want to increase the current then they would need to be in parallel.  If you need to do both then you would make groups that are in series for the desired voltage then connect those groups in parallel until you get the desired current.

author: @francismusso7644
text: To add to the conversation, I had some thing interesting and surprising that happened to me a few years ago..   I am a Ham Radio operator.  I had two copper ground rods just out side my ham shack window.  One was a 4 foot rod and one was an 8 foot rod.  I decided to connect their #8 wires coming into the house  together in hopes of an improved ground area and better grounding effects...  AS soon as I touched the wires together to bond them there was a loud snap and I got one hell of a shock...    I never took this any further but think the two different length ground rods had a different ground potential and in essence a potential difference between them...   Any knowledgeable Ham knows that if you have a potential difference you get a voltage and a current flow when they come together.     I say forget the copper and the Zinc  and work with the different lengths of copper 2 to 1, 3 to 1  length variance and take some measurements to see what is happening here......   Just sharing ideas here for maybe some thing to be developed that will help humanity with cheaper power.     The best to all.

Just because we currently do not understand something does not mean it is not a possibility.

author: @robgad2271
text:
Good info, but you should do a voice over on this one, too much noise and low mic to understand you.

I have been studying this phenomenon for around 50 years. The "earth battery" effect is actually a combination of many things., all of them can be used for electrical power.  A standard probe in the average soil will read a small voltage between two probes.  Extend this down, adding an antenna pickup coil to the circuit and you will find the total available power is not much further down than one meter.

Of the various types of electrical stimulation are effects of Earth's magnetic flux, the soil chemistry, bio energy from microbes, some plants, Earth's harmonic frequency can also be evaluated as a low level power output like any other frequency generated in an item or in the atmosphere.  Soil moisture does not play a large part in this, though absolute dry or absolute wet (as in flooded) will give lower power output.

I use a 8mm wooden dowel one meter long, with a #10 bare copper along the length of the staff, taped on at the ends with 1 CM sticking up to attach to. Near the middle I begin and wind three coils, wired in parallel and one lead extended to the top copper wire attachment, the other covered and extended to the top as well.  In one post hole I bury this arrangement with the leads exposed at the top, using clean back fill with all rocks removed.  1/2 M away I place a galvanized steel rod 1.2 M into the ground and make a row of receivers and ground reference anchors in a long row.  At the receiver I wire these together with a diode and a small electrolytic capacitor to a common wire.  All of the row are wired to two parallel insulated wires and more circuits and components are placed between the rows,   The rows collect the same voltage, usually less than  +1.5 to 3.1 DC volts and the many receivers in each long row collect and add to the amperage potential.   There is more to the details, but the end product from the 1/4 acre I have wired produces an average of +15 volts DC at 30 Amps.  All of my wires are just under the ground level where normal mowing is possible, the electronics are soldered and coated in layering lacquer before burial.  This field is much larger than a few good solar panels on the roof, but it also produces 24-7 and maintains my emergency power backup through a normal 30 Amp solar charger controller and four 1,000 Ah 6 V deep cycle batteries and a 3,000 Watt inverter.  This is good available power backup for some lighting, communication gear and the refrigerator.

My primary power system is an elaborate 15KW over unity system, which is not a store but a direct powered generator system for my all electric house and shop.  This coming spring I will add to the field and do a full video and links to this free system.

I propose an efficient use for this type of battery may be in parallel with another generator, like a micro wind turbine: the voltage from the Earth battery keeps the diodes open in your rectifier, increasing usable power, even in low wind conditions? I wish to try it.

My hypothesis: The Earth battery works similarly to the rain fuel generator; Different regions of moisture and charge within the soil act as the singular rain drop does. Prediction: different coating(s) of the electrodes in conductive ink (or similar) and/or polyurethane (or similar) will increase power. I want to experiment with various large surface area aluminum foil anodes on the south side of a freestanding plastic (electrically isolated) garden box and graphite rod cathodes on the north. Add two or three Daisy wind generators, and I might have a trickle (or better) 12v battery charger. This YouTube channel rocks!

author: @maysucks
text: I had read an article years ago about telegraph lines that were generating their own power, enough power that workers had received a good shock even though the power supply was off. The explanation was that it was similar to a river. The river being the electricity wanted to get from point a to point b to achieve equilibrium, but had to follow a tortuous winding path. The telegraph line produced a straight line, like a canal that allowed a less restrictive path. Interestingly when these telegraph lines fell out of favor and had been cut for some period of time the effect was gone. As though the potential had found a different path. There were other examples of this phenomenon. I chalked it all up to an internet story like the 200mpg carburetor. Maybe there was more to it?

author: @danratsnapnames
text: dissimilar metals affect.   the affect of two types of metals between a semi-conductive medium creates electricity.  similar to a potato battery,.  believe it or not, this works better with Moist earth because it increases the conductivity between the metals.  also orientation to magnetic earth has little affect, but orientation to the anode and cathode shapes does have a great affect.. for instance a larger anode than cathode can create a larger negative pull and the opposite for positive pull..  please keep in mind when taking readings of power, please include both Volts and AMPS.. so that you can get WATTS value.  the WATTS is the WORK that can be used.

replies.author: @danratsnapnames
replies.text: also the composition  of your earth makes a big difference.   the more IRON in your soil, the more conductivity.   or if you try and do this in Florida, it will likely not work well because all of Florida is SAND.  or if you do this in Arizona,  you'll likely get good results due to the high IRON content in the earth,  but only if its wet.  however, being arizona is a desert, it likely wont be wet for long.  not sure about the soil where you are.. but likely has an IRON content.   IRON + Zinc + Copper = very good dissimilar metal reaction = better power generation.   also if you used H2SO4  or battery acid to wet your soil between the poles you'll get MUCH better results.  this is still a BATTERY..  not a power generation..   as for the Debate your referring to,  not likely in a REAL scientific community, but more a backyard science community.

author: @magnetosworld6311
text:
Great presentation.
Might I suggest that you use a 4ft copper and stainless steel rods?
The upper layer of the earth on how dry is really superficial.
Boring in a 4ft copper rod by a creek as the positive and the stainless steel rod by your house as the negative.
Let's say that the creek is a hundred yards away from the house.
Bead a wire from the creek and one by your house.
The positive line will be inches of touching the negative stainless steel rod.
Coil up a 1ft tube of copper wire then exit to attach the wires to a pulse motor.
Place a narrower coiled up tube to fit in the larger one.
Volt test the coil.
And see what you get 😁

author: @richardsandwell2285
text:
I am very interested in low energy but elegant systems like this, I bought an original wind up radio that Trevor Baylis developed, the sound quality is great, and I run it every day in my side conservatory off a 6"x4" little Solar panel because it accepts a wide 3-9 volts DC in.
It only works during daylight, but the background noise gives the appearance someone is home, and I am so lazy I do not have to bother switching it on or off.
I am going to build an Earth Battery Magnesium blocks one end and Stainless Steel plate the other end, then I will use a Joule Thief to up the volts and store it in old emergency lighting NiCds or a Super cap, then maybe run LEDs of it at night with a light sensor MOSFET circuit. 
Another thing I want to do is power my 40 watt Pond pump directly from a Solar panel through a voltage regulator and into a 12-230volt inverter, having some difficulty at the moment getting high amperage low drop out regulators or pre-made circuits but it will happen.

author: @johnlamarca9439
text: I got lucky. I was living in a wilderness area for 14 years. Not by choice. My health dept WITHHELD my birth certificate for 25 years... No BC =s no ID CARD =s no "mark of the beast" everyone else already has and takes for granted... All talk and no listen people all around me insisting my homelessness was "my own fault" without even bothering to know my name or ANYTHING about me. Gangs run the streets in Southern California... A campsite was my solution to safety and privacy. I LEARNED how to live in the woods during those years. As I learned more, my need for money and camping things from the store decreased... While clearing leaves I discovered a zinc earth deposit and a lava rock deposit within 10 feet of each other. Lava rock has lots of iron in it... I went to the hardware store and bought two 4 foot rebar.. I hammered one into the iron and the other into the zinc. Started with very thin wire. Long story short I had electricity from the ground... I didn't know what it was called. Now I know. It's called an "earth battery" or is it a generator? I didn't know how deep the iron and zinc deposits went... If they were shallow, I would have had no electricity AT ALL.. It was COOL charging my phone. Listening to entertainment not worrying about a utility bill while homeless in a wilderness area... Better than fighting over an available plug at Starbucks... 2 lawyers couldn't get my birth certificate... An outreach worker finally got it for me! An outreach worker is not a social worker... Anyone can simply volunteer to help the homeless and instantly become an outreach worker!!

author: @eldjr1104
text:
As Earth's magnetic field dynamically circulates between the magnetic poles, the 1st Law of Thermodynamics demands that a counter-flow equal to that moving AROUND the Earth flow back THROUGH the Earth. That's what he's tapping into, and that's why the electrodes must be oriented parallel to that flow. Earth is a higher-resistance conductor than any refined metallic conductor material, so the electricity which is picked up by the Earth Battery array 'chooses' to flow through the lower-resistance circuit. It's really simple physics. This works until the resistance presented by the LOAD on the circuit at least equals that of the soil media; then the Earth Battery is "dead' or the resistance-to-potential ratio is at 1:1 equilibrium or 'maxxed out'. Simple tuning of the array would involve reading the orientation of the local magnetic polar alignment [a sensitive magnetic compass should suffice] and distributing 'parallel' arrays along that magnetic longitudinal in order to raise the capacity of the array. How udeful this power might be depends upon how many arrays can be put in parallel and upon the power requirements of any system thus powered.

He invented and sold these ideas to others so they could do the scale up and sales as he did not want to spend his time and effort bringing these things to market. Anyway I digress. His plan for the towers was a childhood dream of his. He wanted to wirelessly transmit the energy to his vision of flying machines. Yes flying machines. He wanted to be the 1st man to fly and he would of probably succeeded to If he didnt let his electric motor that was like many of his ideas ahead of his time. He failed this because of a couple of factors. Including the war and mans obsession with petrol potors, the material he was using was not capable to  the torque from the massive rpm he managed to achieve as suitable materials including alloys were not sufficient at that time. But probably the main thing that made him fail to achieve his dream was the massive pr pushback from oil company's and others to stain his reputation as they thought wrongly that he wanted to give energy away for free. He was truly ahead of his time and like most people who were geniuses there intellect and influence would not be recognised to many years after their death. Just think of the advances he brought to the modern world. Truly an amazing man

replies.author: @AnalystPrime
replies.text:
It makes a nice experiment for kids who might get interested in becoming scientists or engineers. Now we have developed LEDs and other electronics that work on very low amount of energy it has actually become useful again, however, between batteries that can last for years and cheap PV cells and other energy sources that produce more power, the list of applications where using a much larger amount of metal and needing more work digging it into the ground is cheaper or otherwise more viable is not that long.
Still, couple of metal rods stuck in the ground seems to be sturdier, much less likely to be stolen or vandalized, and easier to replace option than a solar panel or wind turbine. Also less affected by heat and cold or likely to leak toxic chemicals if eaten by wildlife than batteries.
Rest is basic math: when you need to make a million devices, is a $2 battery with $10 PV panel cheaper than $10 of metal and extra hour of installation work?

This reminds me of another experiment I saw a gentleman doing that was similar, he ran a long wire looped over itself a bunch of times, with AC voltage running through it, like an extension cord, and was able to gain voltage off of the coiled up extention cord, thought that was cool.
He also experimented with long runs of straight wire and was able to get power from them, running horizontally like on a fence line, not sure how that one was working.
But I've got a old well pipe in the back yard that's about 30' feet from another buried zinc 2" pipe, got me thinking about connecting them and seeing what happens.. 🤔 they are east-west oriented though.
Also reminds me of some experiments done on iron fencing and flordalie pattern, some guy was testing just shapes of metal, like the flordalie and getting a small reading off of them, proposing old iron fences might have had a purpose originally, so much of history is forgotten and rewritten, it deffinatly  seems like were going 'Devo'

replies.author: @richardsandwell2285
replies.text:
@aaronedvalson104 Yes boatyards on freshwater areas with Steel boats use Magnesium Anodes welded to the hulls, they are usually cut off and replaced when worn away only 75 to 50% of the original size.
These comprise of a Magnesium ingot cast around a flat piece of Mild Steel running through the centre, 
the Steel, of course, being the bit welded to the Boat Hull to make a strong connection and provide galvanic protection. 
Boatyards will often give the old ones away or charge you a small beer fee because they are virtually worthless for scrap value.
Boats on the coast in Saltwater tend to use Zinc Anodes instead of Magnesium.

author: @bernardstokes6550
text: Some observations....In my set up ...small Copper sheet  north,   Iron rod  rod  south... approx 700mm apart . Volt meter connected with a 1,5k ohm resistor in series. Typical voltage =0.27v ...Here is the odd thing....When facing west if I put my right hand over the resistor connection the voltage increased consistently by 0.05 volts but if I put my left hand over the junction the voltage dropped by a similar amount...face the opposite way round looking east and the effect was on the opposite hand...so maintaining the orientation. Soil is wet, acidic and clay in nature. Clothes included a coat of synthetic material which may be part of the explanation. ...Just thought I would share.

author: @petrh.4198
text:
Robert you did measure several microAmps with 1kOhm resistor? Something like 10microAmp I assume. When I corelate with Ohm law: Current = 0,5V/1000Ohm = 0,001A (1000microAmp) I get multiplication of 100x (between ideal Ohm law and your measurement). One LED needs about 20mA. We then need to connect 4x in serie 0,5V to get 2V and 2000 rows in paralel to get 20miliAmps from 10microAmp. This arrangement could supply one LED (2V, 20mA).
My opinion is, that we are talking about galvanic electricity generated from alcalic metals in a soil of certain pH. That supply would last somewhere between one day and one month.
Not bad for fun! I'll definitely try this tomorow! :-)

author: @Baigle1
text: The magnetic field fluctuates naturally. USGS uses magnetotellurics to map underground lava tunnels in places like Yellowstone National Park. The liquid rock disturbs or blocks the natural every-couple-minute fluctuations from what I remember. When you have a conductive material, like the wet ground between your probes, and a changing magnetic field, a current will be generated. You can take advantage of the electrical properties of the materials (cathode and anode) to pick up some of the electricity due to their galvanic differentials and the differential voltage in the soil. This is my limited understanding of the phenomenon and electric properties. I would rather pick up fluctuations with several long runs of overhead wire, as you might get more energy that way. Don't tie that into your transformer 'isolated' home circuit, in case there is a solar flare which violently moves around the earth's magnetic field.

author: @ItsCalilum
text:
If you wrap a small piece of baking paper around a blade of grass and wrap a small piece of aluminium foil around the baking paper, you can measure a potential difference between the earth and the foil.
If you're interested I can share a simple chemical vapour deposition method you can use to coat the baking paper. This coated paper wrapped around your finger, with foil wrapped around it, gives a potential difference between the foil and any of the fingers on your other hand of about 1.2V . When I connect myself in series with another simple copper/aluminium cell made in the same fashion, I can light an LED.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is awesome mate and i am very interested - can you share?

replies.author: @ItsCalilum
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering I'd be glad to.
To keep it short I'll just explain the basic process as I do it.
Thin plastic disposable food container with flat lid measuring approx. 15 x 22 cm, 4-5 cm deep dish. ( just because I find this a good size, any size is ok as long as volume of ingredients is adjusted proportionally )
Baking paper. Place the lid lengthwise across the width of the baking paper and cut the strip wide enough so that when it's placed against the inside of the lid and the lid is closed upon the dish, there is a small edge of paper outside the lid along each side and a few cm's at each end, you want a full 'paper' seal between the lid/dish.
Take a roll of aluminium foil and cut off a strip twice as wide as the piece of baking paper. Tear it up into 8-10 pieces and scrunch each piece up. ( some fun can be had here with how and how much you scrunch it up or fold it, so play with that )
Boiling water, in this size container I use one full coffee mug.
Place the pieces of foil in the dish and pour in the boiling water. Close the lid with the paper between it and the dish.
While the jug is boiling cut another small piece of foil ¬5cm square and use it as a pouch for one heaped teaspoon of Sodium Hydroxide.
Lift one corner of the lid, drop in the pouch, close lid quickly, place a text book on top, be careful not to breathe any escaping gasses, do in well ventilated area.
Without the text book on top you'll see when the paper becomes transparent, you can pull the lid off remove paper and put another piece in, add a little more foil to keep the reaction going. . .

Trim the uncoated edges off the paper after you've given the coated side a rinse under the tap.
There's other prep you can do on the paper at this stage, but it's not necessary to get working results.

You should now be able to measure a potential across the coated surface of the paper.
Use the paper as a membrane in construction of a simple cell.
For example, I'll cut a strip of foil a little longer than the strip of paper and then lay the paper on the foil slightly offset and beginning before the foil. Then wind a little coil or use a little thin strip of copper and roll it up in the paper/foil.
¬1.2V and lots of interesting properties. (useit/watchit/useit before you try charging it)
Different metals yield different results, obviously, but there's more to that..

There's a relationship between capacity and surface area, can be flat / folded / rolled / stacked

I could go on , but I hope that's a good enough start for you  :-)

author: @alasdair4161
text: That reminds me of a way I used to run my garden lights by shunting my street pole ground (against my fence) through two heavy primary turns on a transformer core then on to a huge ground plate I put in under my garden. The current from the pole's neutral ground return took the path to my lower resistance high surface area ground with a small portion of it taking their dry high resistance stake, and my transformer stepped it up 20:1, I ran it through a rectifier and a regulator before powering some small lights. It was hilarious, in winter the lights would come on willy nilly and change brightness endlessly (in sync to neighbours cookers and heaters), but it was always better in summer, when my plate was damp and much lower resistance from being watered, running day and night they totalled about 10 watts and I could have probably achieved more if I had heavier gauge cable to carry the low voltage and high current to the transformer.

author: @RWBHere
text: If you put a pair of electrodes across a seawater river estuary, or a man-made channel containing flowing conductive water, you can measure a magnetohydrodynamic current, depending upon the water flow rate. Try it; it's a genuine effect. In reverse, if you put a voltage across two electrodes, separated by conductive water, the water will flow parallel to the electrodes, and the direction can be reversed by reversing the polarity. The same effect can be used to pump Mercury, molten Sodium, etc.

author: @jjmcrosbie
text:
Sir, 
Thank you for an entertaining video.
1 - If there were free electric energy to be had by placing metal stakes in the ground, all the wire fences fixed on metal stakes would be using it up!  
2 - However, as you were using different metals we may assume you were observing the difference in potential of the two metals.  Now, there is a volt or so to be had between zinc and copper, depending on the electrolyte.  John F Daniel obtained 1.1Volts with an electrolyte of sulphuric acid.  (The copper sulphate only served as a depolariser).  Unfortunately, brass consists of both Copper and zinc, frustrating your attempt to find a voltage.
3 - Poisson d'avril?  En tous cas, portez-vous bien.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: toujours la même chose avec moi - je vais bien. I am glad you liked the video - just for interest - this method was used extensively in the early 19th century - there wasn't a lot of power but it was used for all kinds of things.

replies.author: @jjmcrosbie
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering Sir,
1 - Thank you for your reply.
2 - So wasn't it an April Fool joke?  (Ce n'est pas de poisson d'avril?)
3 - Please can you expand upon its use, and/or give reference to any kind of literature or report on the subject?  V/A/W available and so-on?
4 - On a tangent, have you heard of earth communication?  Theoretically, you can put a pair of stakes in the ground, separated by as much distance as can be managed, and pass a signal between them which can be faintly picked up anywhere on earth using another pair of widely spaced stakes..  And no, I haven't tried it.  I proposed a maritime version which could offer secure communications for a Naval application.  They passed it to a fresh graduate scientist who knew much less than I did, and he poo-pooed it out of hand.  He didn't even look at the details I supplied.  And it wasn't pursued.
Incidentally, re earth communication, which has been tried and I believe used in very small way, wouldn't they have come across the Earth Battery?  Or does it rely on different metals?
5 - Before we can talk about "free" energy, we must consider that used up in the fabrication of the installation/materials, etc.  In this case, that used in providing the metal rods (Mining, transport, refinement and manufacture).  Triste, n'est-ce pas?  OK, I live in France - I admit it.  You'd love it here!
Portez-vous bien.  Salut, Clive.

PS, I recently saw this on youtube, purportedly from Robert Murray-Smith: 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=mInrDzF5kOU&t=163s
I wondered if there's a connection.  Luke appears to operate in a similar spirit to yourself.  I put a few (I hope) helpful remarks in the comments.
PPS  I made a Daniel cell at home during my school days. The sulphuric acid was quite quickly turned into zinc sulphate but the cell continued to produce the same V/A/W.  However, I found that a saline electrolyte produces a whole different set of potentials between metals.  I haven't researched that further however.

author: @MrFmiller
text:
I’ve experimented with earth currents. The changes in power from various materials indicates at least part of the power generated is electrochemical.
The depth and composition of the “electrodes”, mineral makeup of the soil, pH, moisture content, and any number of other variables make a difference.
I used stainless rods to minimize electrochemical reactions.
There may be additive or cancelling currents as well. After experimenting for days in several locations I never did come up with definitive criteria. Inserting the spike as little as an additional inch or moving it a foot raised or lowered the voltage sometimes arbitrarily. Sometimes the power changed with moisture but not always. Sometimes it went up and sometimes it went down. I think it just depends on what the makeup of the subsurface materials and stratification, as well as the cumulative contact area between the various subsurface materials and the rods is at any given location. Like you did in the video, manipulating the rods produced variations. 
It was frustrating but fun.

replies.text: I agree mate - the main point here is to have fun

author: @m3sca1
text: Best to play with steel wire since the magnetic current can flow through it. Touching a steel wire across 2 earth rods will bring the magnetism through the wire that previously was not magnetic. I was recently playing with rods and PVC coated steel wire. Also try using old LED modules to pump voltage through the earth...i was getting 18v off a LED array(PV effect) that when put through the earth came back with an extra volt, across about 7 meters of earth. Curious indeed. Where did the extra volt come from? I used high grade stainless rods from hot water pump shafts as earth rods so not galvanic!!!!!!

author: @Xeno_Bardock
text: Earths crust acts like a charged dielectric material of a capacitor. By inserting various metal rods into the earths crust, you create positive and negative terminals and by using different metals for terminals, you create potential difference, the higher the potential difference between different metals, the higher the volts and amps. If large area of land is used and various metals are connected in series and parallel combination, i think good amount of free electricity can be harvested from earth which then can be rectified to DC and fed to inverter. One part of this free electricity is from ground connection of power lines, other part is from ground rod of AM radio station and other part of free electricity is from birkeland currents from the Sun which enter the earth at the poles so in earth battery you are basically tapping into these three sources. One source, the birkeland currents is true free electricity that earth receives from the cosmos. Birkeland current is what powers all the stars and galaxies and planets.

replies.author: @Xeno_Bardock
replies.text: Youtube search "Top 10 Reasons The Universe is Electric" and "Birkeland Currents Explained" if you are interesting in researching electric universe theory.

author: @kreynolds1123
text:
When looking for telluric currents Use the same material for both the anode and the cathode to isolate out galvonic reaction.  You'll need seperation distance to have electrical potential. And you should try placing electrodes in a circle equal distance from a center electrode as youll find one electrode on the circle has the highest voltage, and another will have the lowest voltage with respect to the center electrode. Throughout the day 
You'll find that the direction of current will change.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: awesome - cheers mate

replies.author: @grantmcauliffe3437
replies.text: Yes. If it is different metals, then it is galvanic.

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text: @grantmcauliffe3437 Well potentially telluric electric potentials adding to or subtracting from galvanic potentials.

replies.author: @grantmcauliffe3437
replies.text: @kreynolds1123 I agree with you. That would be a way to potentially measure it. Pun was unintentional. It would be easier to simply use the same metal, though.

replies.author: @kreynolds1123
replies.text: @grantmcauliffe3437 yup same metal, isolates for telluric potentials.

replies.author: @rebeccashetter2309
replies.text: That makes sense; mushrooms root system connects an entire garden and that would be pulling energy from the earth and feeds the other plants with energy. All living animals and plants are both biological and electrical with radiation. The problem with harvesting that energy; would it kill or weaken the plants? I know there is a method of planting fruit trees that suggest digging a large hole and putting stone into the ground and then lacing cooper wire into the soil with the tree. This has been found to increase the growth and health of the tree. This is all very interesting... and may come in handy to know in these times. Stay safe everyone and keep learning and keep sharing.
